toolUseResult.originalFile:
resource "aws_ssm_document" "this" {

  name            = "${var.name}-deploy"
  document_type   = "Command"
  document_format = "YAML"

  content = <<-DOC
    schemaVersion: "2.2"
    description: "Deploy OpenClaw application config from S3 and restart containers"
    parameters:
      region:
        type: String
        default: "${data.aws_region.current.id}"
    mainSteps:
      - action: aws:runShellScript
        name: deployOpenclaw
        inputs:
          runCommand:
            - "#!/bin/bash"
            - "set -euo pipefail"
            - "exec > /var/log/openclaw-deploy.log 2>&1"
            - ""
            - "OPENCLAW_HOME=/home/openclaw"
            - "CONFIG_BUCKET=${var.config_bucket_name}"
            - "INSTANCE_NAME=${var.name}"
            - "DOMAIN=${var.aws_route53_zone_domain}"
            - "REGION={{ region }}"
            - ""
            - "# Pull config from S3"
            - "aws s3 sync \"s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME/\" --region \"$REGION\" --delete --exclude '.env*' --exclude 'config/agents/*' --exclude 'config/credentials/*' --exclude 'config/devices/*' --exclude 'config/logs/*' --exclude 'config/sessions/*' --exclude 'config/identity/*' --exclude 'config/status-announcement.json' --exclude 'workspace/*' --exclude 'letsencrypt/*'"
            - ""
            - "# Ensure directory structure"
            - "mkdir -p $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/{devices,logs,cron,canvas,sessions,identity,bin,credentials/google-workspace} $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace"
            - "chmod +x $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/*.sh 2>/dev/null || true"
            - "test -f $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/status-announcement.json || echo '{}' > $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/status-announcement.json"
            - ""
            - "# Deploy managed workspace files from S3"
            - "aws s3 cp \"s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/workspace/MODEL_GUIDE.md\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/MODEL_GUIDE.md\" --region \"$REGION\" 2>/dev/null || true"
            - "aws s3 cp \"s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/workspace/STATUS-PAGE-INSTRUCTIONS.md\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/STATUS-PAGE-INSTRUCTIONS.md\" --region \"$REGION\" 2>/dev/null || true"
            - ""
            - "# Install systemd services"
            - "cp $OPENCLAW_HOME/approvals/openclaw-approvals.service /etc/systemd/system/"
            - "cp $OPENCLAW_HOME/heartbeat/openclaw-heartbeat.service /etc/systemd/system/"
            - "cp $OPENCLAW_HOME/heartbeat/openclaw-heartbeat.timer /etc/systemd/system/"
            - ""
            - "# Fetch secrets from SSM"
            - "GATEWAY_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/gateway-token --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text)"
            - "LLM_API_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/llm-api-key --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/telegram-bot-token --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/discord-bot-token --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "APPROVALS_PASSWORD=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/approvals-password --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-workspace-token-key --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-id --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-secret --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - ""
            - "# Write container env file"
            - "printf 'OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\\nOPENCLAW_DOMAIN=%s\\nAWS_REGION=%s\\nINSTANCE_NAME=%s\\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=%s\\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=%s\\nDISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=%s\\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=%s\\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=%s\\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=%s\\n' \"$GATEWAY_TOKEN\" \"$DOMAIN\" \"$REGION\" \"$INSTANCE_NAME\" \"$LLM_API_KEY\" \"$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN\" \"$DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\" \"$GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY\" \"$GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID\" \"$GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET\" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env"
            - ""
            - "# Write approvals env file"
            - "printf 'APPROVALS_PASSWORD=%s\\nDEVICES_DIR=%s/config/devices\\n' \"$APPROVALS_PASSWORD\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME\" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals"
            - "chmod 600 $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals"
            - ""
            - "# Set ownership"
            - "chown -R openclaw:openclaw $OPENCLAW_HOME"
            - "chown -R 1000:1000 $OPENCLAW_HOME/config $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace"
            - ""
            - "# Start containers"
            - "cd $OPENCLAW_HOME"
            - "sudo -u openclaw docker compose pull --quiet"
            - "sudo -u openclaw docker compose up -d --force-recreate"
            - "docker image prune -af"
            - ""
            - "# Wait for gateway to be healthy before installing skills"
            - "for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker exec openclaw-gateway curl -sf http://localhost:18789/health && break || sleep 2; done"
            - ""
            - "# Install/update clawhub skills"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install panthrocorp-google-workspace --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for panthrocorp-google-workspace'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install arnarsson-git-essentials --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for arnarsson-git-essentials'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install michael-laffin-markdown-formatter --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for michael-laffin-markdown-formatter'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install steipete-markdown-converter --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for steipete-markdown-converter'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install whiteknight07-exa-web-search-free --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for whiteknight07-exa-web-search-free'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install tag-assistant-github-cli --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for tag-assistant-github-cli'"
            - ""
            - "# Restart CloudWatch Agent with latest config"
            - "if [[ -x /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl && -f $OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json ]]; then /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl -a fetch-config -m ec2 -s -c file:$OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json; fi"
            - ""
            - "# Start approvals service and heartbeat timer"
            - "systemctl daemon-reload"
            - "systemctl enable openclaw-approvals"
            - "systemctl restart openclaw-approvals"
            - "systemctl enable --now openclaw-heartbeat.timer"
            - ""
            - "echo Deploy complete."
            - "docker ps"
  DOC
}

            - "# Write container env file"

            - "APPROVALS_PASSWORD=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/approvals-password --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-workspace-token-key --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-id --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-secret --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - ""
            - "# Write container env file"
            - "printf 'OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\\nOPENCLAW_DOMAIN=%s\\nAWS_REGION=%s\\nINSTANCE_NAME=%s\\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=%s\\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=%s\\nDISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=%s\\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=%s\\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=%s\\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=%s\\n' \"$GATEWAY_TOKEN\" \"$DOMAIN\" \"$REGION\" \"$INSTANCE_NAME\" \"$LLM_API_KEY\" \"$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN\" \"$DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\" \"$GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY\" \"$GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID\" \"$GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET\" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env"
            - ""
            - "# Write approvals env file"
            - "printf 'APPROVALS_PASSWORD=%s\\nDEVICES_DIR=%s/config/devices\\n' \"$APPROVALS_PASSWORD\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME\" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals"
            - "chmod 600 $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals"
            - ""
            - "# Set ownership"
            - "chown -R openclaw:openclaw $OPENCLAW_HOME"
            - "chown -R 1000:1000 $OPENCLAW_HOME/config $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace"
            - ""
            - "# Start containers"
            - "cd $OPENCLAW_HOME"
            - "sudo -u openclaw docker compose pull --quiet"
            - "sudo -u openclaw docker compose up -d --force-recreate"
            - "docker image prune -af"
            - ""
            - "# Wait for gateway to be healthy before installing skills"
            - "for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker exec openclaw-gateway curl -sf http://localhost:18789/health && break || sleep 2; done"
            - ""
            - "# Install/update clawhub skills"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install panthrocorp-google-workspace --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for panthrocorp-google-workspace'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install arnarsson-git-essentials --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for arnarsson-git-essentials'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install michael-laffin-markdown-formatter --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for michael-laffin-markdown-formatter'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install steipete-markdown-converter --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for steipete-markdown-converter'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install whiteknight07-exa-web-search-free --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for whiteknight07-exa-web-search-free'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install tag-assistant-github-cli --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for tag-assistant-github-cli'"
            - ""
            - "# Restart CloudWatch Agent with latest config"
            - "if [[ -x /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl && -f $OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json ]]; then /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl -a fetch-config -m ec2 -s -c file:$OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json; fi"
            - ""
            - "# Start approvals service and heartbeat timer"
            - "systemctl daemon-reload"
            - "systemctl enable openclaw-approvals"
            - "systemctl restart openclaw-approvals"
            - "systemctl enable --now openclaw-heartbeat.timer"
            - ""
            - "echo Deploy complete."
            - "docker ps"
  DOC
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
resource "aws_ssm_document" "this" {

  name            = "${var.name}-deploy"
  document_type   = "Command"
  document_format = "YAML"

  content = <<-DOC
    schemaVersion: "2.2"
    description: "Deploy OpenClaw application config from S3 and restart containers"
    parameters:
      region:
        type: String
        default: "${data.aws_region.current.id}"
    mainSteps:
      - action: aws:runShellScript
        name: deployOpenclaw
        inputs:
          runCommand:
            - "#!/bin/bash"
            - "set -euo pipefail"
            - "exec > /var/log/openclaw-deploy.log 2>&1"
            - ""
            - "OPENCLAW_HOME=/home/openclaw"
            - "CONFIG_BUCKET=${var.config_bucket_name}"
            - "INSTANCE_NAME=${var.name}"
            - "DOMAIN=${var.aws_route53_zone_domain}"
            - "REGION={{ region }}"
            - ""
            - "# Pull config from S3"
            - "aws s3 sync \"s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME/\" --region \"$REGION\" --delete --exclude '.env*' --exclude 'config/agents/*' --exclude 'config/credentials/*' --exclude 'config/devices/*' --exclude 'config/logs/*' --exclude 'config/sessions/*' --exclude 'config/identity/*' --exclude 'config/status-announcement.json' --exclude 'workspace/*' --exclude 'letsencrypt/*'"
            - ""
            - "# Ensure directory structure"
            - "mkdir -p $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/{devices,logs,cron,canvas,sessions,identity,bin,credentials/google-workspace,credentials/zoho-mail} $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace"
            - "chmod +x $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/*.sh 2>/dev/null || true"
            - "test -f $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/status-announcement.json || echo '{}' > $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/status-announcement.json"
            - ""
            - "# Deploy managed workspace files from S3"
            - "aws s3 cp \"s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/workspace/MODEL_GUIDE.md\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/MODEL_GUIDE.md\" --region \"$REGION\" 2>/dev/null || true"
            - "aws s3 cp \"s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/workspace/STATUS-PAGE-INSTRUCTIONS.md\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/STATUS-PAGE-INSTRUCTIONS.md\" --region \"$REGION\" 2>/dev/null || true"
            - ""
            - "# Install systemd services"
            - "cp $OPENCLAW_HOME/approvals/openclaw-approvals.service /etc/systemd/system/"
            - "cp $OPENCLAW_HOME/heartbeat/openclaw-heartbeat.service /etc/systemd/system/"
            - "cp $OPENCLAW_HOME/heartbeat/openclaw-heartbeat.timer /etc/systemd/system/"
            - ""
            - "# Fetch secrets from SSM"
            - "GATEWAY_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/gateway-token --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text)"
            - "LLM_API_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/llm-api-key --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/telegram-bot-token --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/discord-bot-token --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "APPROVALS_PASSWORD=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/approvals-password --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-workspace-token-key --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-id --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-secret --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "ZOHO_CLIENT_ID=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/zoho-client-id --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/zoho-client-secret --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - "ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name /$INSTANCE_NAME/zoho-mail-token-key --region $REGION --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
            - ""
            - "# Write container env file"
            - "printf 'OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\\nOPENCLAW_DOMAIN=%s\\nAWS_REGION=%s\\nINSTANCE_NAME=%s\\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=%s\\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=%s\\nDISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=%s\\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=%s\\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=%s\\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=%s\\n' \"$GATEWAY_TOKEN\" \"$DOMAIN\" \"$REGION\" \"$INSTANCE_NAME\" \"$LLM_API_KEY\" \"$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN\" \"$DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\" \"$GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY\" \"$GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID\" \"$GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET\" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env"
            - ""
            - "# Write approvals env file"
            - "printf 'APPROVALS_PASSWORD=%s\\nDEVICES_DIR=%s/config/devices\\n' \"$APPROVALS_PASSWORD\" \"$OPENCLAW_HOME\" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals"
            - "chmod 600 $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals"
            - ""
            - "# Set ownership"
            - "chown -R openclaw:openclaw $OPENCLAW_HOME"
            - "chown -R 1000:1000 $OPENCLAW_HOME/config $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace"
            - ""
            - "# Start containers"
            - "cd $OPENCLAW_HOME"
            - "sudo -u openclaw docker compose pull --quiet"
            - "sudo -u openclaw docker compose up -d --force-recreate"
            - "docker image prune -af"
            - ""
            - "# Wait for gateway to be healthy before installing skills"
            - "for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker exec openclaw-gateway curl -sf http://localhost:18789/health && break || sleep 2; done"
            - ""
            - "# Install/update clawhub skills"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install panthrocorp-google-workspace --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for panthrocorp-google-workspace'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install arnarsson-git-essentials --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for arnarsson-git-essentials'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install michael-laffin-markdown-formatter --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for michael-laffin-markdown-formatter'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install steipete-markdown-converter --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for steipete-markdown-converter'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install whiteknight07-exa-web-search-free --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for whiteknight07-exa-web-search-free'"
            - "docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install tag-assistant-github-cli --force 2>&1 || echo 'WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for tag-assistant-github-cli'"
            - ""
            - "# Restart CloudWatch Agent with latest config"
            - "if [[ -x /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl && -f $OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json ]]; then /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl -a fetch-config -m ec2 -s -c file:$OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json; fi"
            - ""
            - "# Start approvals service and heartbeat timer"
            - "systemctl daemon-reload"
            - "systemctl enable openclaw-approvals"
            - "systemctl restart openclaw-approvals"
            - "systemctl enable --now openclaw-heartbeat.timer"
            - ""
            - "echo Deploy complete."
            - "docker ps"
  DOC
}

content.text: All six files done. Let me update the CLAUDE.md to document the installed skill, and save a memory entry.

content.content:
230	#### google-workspace (panthrocorp/openclaw-skills)
231	
232	Custom skill providing read-only Gmail, configurable Calendar, and read-only Contacts access. Published to clawhub as `panthrocorp-google-workspace`.
233	
234	| Service | OAuth Scope | Mode |
235	|---------|------------|------|
236	| Gmail | `gmail.readonly` | Read-only (hardcoded, no write code paths) |
237	| Calendar | `calendar.readonly` or `calendar.events` | Configurable: `off`, `readonly`, `readwrite` |
238	| Contacts | `contacts.readonly` | Read-only (hardcoded, no write code paths) |
239	
240	OAuth tokens encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) at `config/credentials/google-workspace/`. Encryption key from Bitwarden SM via SSM Parameter Store.
241	
242	One-time OAuth setup after deploy:
243	```bash
244	ssh -i ~/.ssh/openclaw-operator ubuntu@openclaw.listell.co.uk
245	sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway google-workspace config set --gmail=true --calendar=readonly --contacts=true
246	sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway google-workspace auth login
247	```
248	
249	#### git-essentials (arnarsson)
250	
251	Markdown-only reference skill for Git workflows. No binary, no API access, no permissions required.
252	
253	| | |
254	|---|---|
255	| VirusTotal | Benign |
256	| OpenClaw | Benign (HIGH confidence) |
257	| Risk | None (read-only reference material) |
258

#### google-workspace (panthrocorp/openclaw-skills)

Custom skill providing read-only Gmail, configurable Calendar, and read-only Contacts access. Published to clawhub as `panthrocorp-google-workspace`.

| Service | OAuth Scope | Mode |
|---------|------------|------|
| Gmail | `gmail.readonly` | Read-only (hardcoded, no write code paths) |
| Calendar | `calendar.readonly` or `calendar.events` | Configurable: `off`, `readonly`, `readwrite` |
| Contacts | `contacts.readonly` | Read-only (hardcoded, no write code paths) |

OAuth tokens encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) at `config/credentials/google-workspace/`. Encryption key from Bitwarden SM via SSM Parameter Store.

One-time OAuth setup after deploy:
```bash
ssh -i ~/.ssh/openclaw-operator ubuntu@openclaw.listell.co.uk
sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway google-workspace config set --gmail=true --calendar=readonly --contacts=true
sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway google-workspace auth login
```

#### git-essentials (arnarsson)

Markdown-only reference skill for Git workflows. No binary, no API access, no permissions required.

| | |
|---|---|
| VirusTotal | Benign |
| OpenClaw | Benign (HIGH confidence) |
| Risk | None (read-only reference material) |

## DNS

Route53 hosted zones are created per instance. NS delegation to the parent zone (management account) is managed via Terraform using a cross-account provider alias. The A record is updated by the user data script on boot (points to the EC2 private IP, resolved via Twingate DNS proxy).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project

OpenClaw is an AWS environment for running the OpenClaw AI agent framework in isolation. OpenClaw is treated as hostile software: the infrastructure is designed to contain a full compromise of the EC2 instance within a dedicated, locked-down AWS account. The project supports multiple instances across AWS accounts for different customers. The agent running on this instance is called **Trevor**.

Full architecture rationale is in `.docs/DESIGN.md`. Implementation is tracked via milestone docs in `.docs/M1-*.md` through `.docs/M5-*.md`.

## Terraform

Single root module at `terraform/` with S3 backend and GitHub Actions workflow. The root module uses the flatten-with-defaults pattern (style guide section 4) to deploy multiple instances from a single `var.instances` map.

Provider config uses no `profile`. Auth is environment-driven: `AWS_PROFILE` locally, OIDC in CI.

A cross-account provider alias (`aws.management`) assumes the Route53 delegation role in the management account for NS record creation.

### Running locally

```bash
cd terraform
source ../.env.bitwarden
AWS_PROFILE=panthrocorp-openclaw terraform init
AWS_PROFILE=panthrocorp-openclaw terraform plan \
  -var-file=variables/listell/sandbox-openclaw/sandbox-openclaw.tfvars
```

### Style

See `~/.claude/references/terraform-style-guide.md` for the full style guide. Always consult it when writing Terraform.

### Git workflow

- Never commit directly to `main`. Always use a feature branch and PR.
- Never use `--no-verify` to skip pre-commit hooks. Fix the hook failure instead.
- The deploy workflow runs on merge to main.

## Pre-commit

All Terraform hooks target `^terraform/`. Run manually with:

```bash
pre-commit run --all-files
```

Key gates: `terraform_fmt`, `terraform_validate`, `tflint`, `trivy`, `checkov`, `gitleaks`, `shellcheck`.

Config files live in `.config/` (`.tflint.hcl`, `.checkov.yaml`, `.yamllint.yaml`, `.terraform-docs.yml`, `.trivyignore`).

## Secrets Management

All secrets are stored in **Bitwarden Secrets Manager** (EU region, `PanthroCorp` project). Terraform reads secrets at plan/apply time via the `bitwarden/bitwarden-secrets` provider. No secrets are passed as environment variables to Terraform (except `BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN` for provider auth).

| Secret | Scope | Description |
|--------|-------|-------------|
| `TWINGATE_API_TOKEN` | Global | Twingate provider authentication |
| `TWINGATE_NETWORK` | Global | Twingate network slug |
| `SSH_PUBLIC_KEY_OPENCLAW_OPERATOR` | Per instance | SSH public key for EC2 key pair |
| `OPENROUTER_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_*` | Per instance | OpenRouter API key |
| `TELEGRAM_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_*` | Per instance | Telegram bot token |
| `DISCORD_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_*` | Per instance | Discord bot token |
| `GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_*` | Per instance | AES-256-GCM key for Google OAuth token encryption |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_*` | Per instance | Google Cloud OAuth client ID |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_*` | Per instance | Google Cloud OAuth client secret |

Per-instance secrets are referenced by key name in the tfvars file (e.g. `bitwarden_secret_key_openrouter_api_key`). Terraform looks up the key in Bitwarden SM and retrieves the value.

Locally, source `.env.bitwarden` which exports `BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN` and `TF_VAR_bws_access_token`.

## Variables

Non-sensitive, per-deployment values live in committed tfvars files under `terraform/variables/<customer>/<account>/`. Per-instance configuration is defined in the `instances` map within the tfvars file. See `terraform/terraform.tfvars.example` for the full schema.

Global variables (shared across all instances):
- `aws_region`
- `aws_iam_role_management_delegation_arn`

Per-instance variables are fields within the `instances` map, with defaults applied via the flatten-with-defaults pattern in `terraform/locals.tf`.

## CI/CD

GitHub Actions with OIDC federation (no static credentials):

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — lint on all PRs and pushes
- `.github/workflows/deploy-sandbox-openclaw.yml` — plan on PR, apply + SSM deploy on merge to main

GitHub secrets: `BWS_ACCESS_TOKEN` (the only secret). <!-- pragma: allowlist secret -->

The workflow passes `TF_VAR_bws_access_token` from the GitHub secret and references a committed tfvars file for all other configuration.

## Connecting to the EC2 Instance

Access is via Twingate. The Twingate connector runs as a host-level systemd service on the EC2 instance (installed by the user-data script). No public inbound ports. Zero inbound security group rules.

Shell access (via Twingate, connects as `ubuntu` user):

```bash
ssh -i ~/.ssh/openclaw-operator ubuntu@openclaw.listell.co.uk
```

SSM Session Manager fallback (no Twingate required):

```bash
aws ssm start-session --target $(terraform -chdir=terraform output -json instance_ids | jq -r '."eu-west-2.openclaw"')
```

Web UI: `https://openclaw.listell.co.uk` (HTTPS via Let's Encrypt DNS-01 challenge, accessible through Twingate client).

## OpenClaw Container Image

The image is `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw`, pinned to a specific version tag (not `latest`). The current pinned version is in `config/docker-compose.yml`.

When starting a session, check for newer tags:

```bash
skopeo list-tags docker://ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw | jq '[.Tags[] | select(test("^2026"))] | sort_by(.) | reverse | .[0:5]'
```

If a new version exists, review its release notes before updating.

## Docker Architecture

The deployment uses three containers via Docker Compose:

- `openclaw-gateway` — the OpenClaw agent (binds to `127.0.0.1:18789` inside its network namespace)
- `caddy` — reverse proxy with TLS, shares the gateway's network namespace, exposes ports 80 and 443
- `openclaw-cli` — on-demand CLI for onboarding and diagnostics (profile: `cli`, does not auto-start)

Twingate runs as a host-level systemd service (`twingate-connector.service`), not as a Docker container.

Caddy is required because the gateway binds to localhost inside the container. Without Caddy sharing the network namespace, the gateway is unreachable from the host.

Application config is deployed via SSM Run Command (not baked into user data). Config changes in `config/` trigger a deploy without instance replacement.

### Container permissions

The gateway runs as UID `1000:1000` with a read-only filesystem. Data directories under `config/` (devices, logs, cron, canvas, sessions) must be writable by UID 1000. The SSM deploy document handles this with `chown -R 1000:1000`.

### HTTPS

Caddy serves HTTPS using Let's Encrypt certificates managed by certbot on the host. Certs are stored on the EBS data volume at `/home/openclaw/letsencrypt/` to persist across instance replacement. Certbot uses the DNS-01 challenge via Route53 (no inbound ports needed). A systemd timer renews automatically at 02:30 daily.

## OpenClaw Configuration

Application config lives in `config/openclaw.json` (committed). This file is deployed to the instance via SSM Run Command. It configures the gateway, model, auth, and agent defaults. Interactive onboarding (`openclaw onboard`) is not needed because the config is pre-populated.

### Key settings

- `gateway.bind`: `loopback` (Caddy proxies externally)
- `gateway.auth.mode`: `token` (Caddy injects bearer token via `Authorization` header)
- Network access is via Twingate (connector runs as a host-level systemd service)
- `session.dmScope`: `per-channel-peer` (isolates DM sessions)

LLM models are configured per instance via the `llm_models` map in the tfvars file. Multiple models can be made available simultaneously, with one marked as `primary`. The free-tier nvidia/nemotron model is always included automatically. The gateway token is generated by the SSM deploy script and stored at `/home/openclaw/.gateway-token`. It is not committed to the repo.

### Adding OpenAI Codex (ChatGPT Pro)

If the customer has a ChatGPT Pro subscription, the OpenAI Codex model can be added via OAuth. This is a one-time manual step per instance (OAuth tokens are stored on the data EBS and persist across deploys).

1. Add `"openai-codex/gpt-5.4"` to the `llm_models` map in the tfvars, then `terraform apply` and deploy
2. SSH into the instance via Twingate:
   ```bash
   ssh -i ~/.ssh/openclaw-operator ubuntu@<domain>
   ```
3. Run the onboard command (interactive, requires a browser for OAuth):
   ```bash
   sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway node openclaw.mjs onboard \
     --auth-choice openai-codex --skip-channels --skip-skills --skip-daemon --skip-search --skip-ui
   ```
4. When prompted, copy the URL and open it in your local browser
5. Authenticate with your OpenAI account
6. Copy the redirect URL from the browser address bar and paste it back into the terminal
7. Skip hooks setup when prompted
8. Recreate all containers (Caddy shares the gateway's network namespace, so a simple restart breaks connectivity):
   ```bash
   cd /home/openclaw && sudo -u openclaw docker compose up -d --force-recreate
   ```

OAuth credentials are stored at `/home/openclaw/config/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json` on the data EBS. The S3 sync excludes `config/agents/*` so these are not overwritten by deploys.

### Device pairing

New browser sessions require device pairing. Approve from an SSH session:

```bash
# List pending devices
sudo -u openclaw docker compose -f /home/openclaw/docker-compose.yml run --rm openclaw-cli devices list

# Approve a specific request
sudo -u openclaw docker compose -f /home/openclaw/docker-compose.yml run --rm openclaw-cli devices approve <requestId>
```

### OpenClaw CLI

Run CLI commands via docker compose:

```bash
sudo -u openclaw docker compose -f /home/openclaw/docker-compose.yml run --rm openclaw-cli <command>
```

Useful commands: `devices list`, `devices approve`, `health`, `security audit --deep`, `config get`.

## Security

See `.docs/SECURITY-TODO.md` for the full security checklist. Key points:

- The security guide is at docs.openclaw.ai/gateway/security
- Run `openclaw security audit --deep` regularly
- LLM backend is configurable per instance via OpenRouter (Bedrock IAM policy retained for optional use)
- DM scope is `per-channel-peer` (isolates sessions)
- Control-plane tools should be denied for untrusted surfaces

## Skills

OpenClaw supports installable skills from the Claw Skills registry (`clawskills.sh`). Skills extend Trevor's capabilities but run within the same trust boundary as the agent itself.

**Always assess skills for risk before installation.** Consider:

- What permissions and system access does the skill require?
- Does it introduce new attack surface (network, filesystem, external APIs)?
- What is the VirusTotal and OpenClaw moderation status?
- Could the skill be used to exfiltrate data or pivot to internal resources?
- Is the skill actively maintained and from a reputable author?

Document each installed skill, its version, and the risk assessment outcome in this section.

### Installed skills

#### google-workspace (panthrocorp/openclaw-skills)

Custom skill providing read-only Gmail, configurable Calendar, and read-only Contacts access. Published to clawhub as `panthrocorp-google-workspace`.

| Service | OAuth Scope | Mode |
|---------|------------|------|
| Gmail | `gmail.readonly` | Read-only (hardcoded, no write code paths) |
| Calendar | `calendar.readonly` or `calendar.events` | Configurable: `off`, `readonly`, `readwrite` |
| Contacts | `contacts.readonly` | Read-only (hardcoded, no write code paths) |

OAuth tokens encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) at `config/credentials/google-workspace/`. Encryption key from Bitwarden SM via SSM Parameter Store.

One-time OAuth setup after deploy:
```bash
ssh -i ~/.ssh/openclaw-operator ubuntu@openclaw.listell.co.uk
sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway google-workspace config set --gmail=true --calendar=readonly --contacts=true
sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway google-workspace auth login
```

#### git-essentials (arnarsson)

Markdown-only reference skill for Git workflows. No binary, no API access, no permissions required.

| | |
|---|---|
| VirusTotal | Benign |
| OpenClaw | Benign (HIGH confidence) |
| Risk | None (read-only reference material) |

## DNS

Route53 hosted zones are created per instance. NS delegation to the parent zone (management account) is managed via Terraform using a cross-account provider alias. The A record is updated by the user data script on boot (points to the EC2 private IP, resolved via Twingate DNS proxy).

Last updated: 2026-03-29

Terraform changes merged into this repo to support the `panthrocorp-zoho-mail` clawhub skill for Trevor's dedicated Zoho Mail account (EU data centre).

**Why:** Trevor has a dedicated Zoho Mail account and needs full read/write email access as an agent capability.

**Status:** Infrastructure done, skill not yet published to clawhub. The clawhub install step in ssm_deploy.tf will warn gracefully until the skill is published.

```bash
sudo -u openclaw docker exec -it openclaw-gateway zoho-mail auth login

content.text:
That's already the design - the skill code (in `openclaw-skills`) just reads env vars. `ZOHO_CLIENT_ID`, `ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY` come from `process.env`. No Bitwarden SDK, no vault calls in the skill itself.

The Bitwarden wiring in this repo (`locals.tf`, `ssm.tf` etc.) is PanthroCorp's deployment-specific mechanism for populating those env vars. Another user on a different vault would just set the same env vars through their own mechanism. The skill stays vault-agnostic.

Same applies to google-workspace - the skill reads `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY` from env. It has no knowledge of how those values arrived.

Nothing needs to change in the infrastructure for that — the contract is simply "these env vars must be present at container startup". The skill just documents which vars it expects.

Ready to raise the PR when you are. Do you want to tackle the Zoho API console setup and Bitwarden entries first, or should I open the PR now and you can unblock it in parallel?

The infrastructure wiring in this repo is complete — the six Terraform files are updated. But the skill code itself doesn't exist yet.

The skill (`panthrocorp-zoho-mail`) needs to be built in the `panthrocorp/openclaw-skills` repo — that's where google-workspace lives. Until it's published to clawhub, the install line in `ssm_deploy.tf` will hit the warning fallback and no-op.

To summarise what's done vs what's pending:

**Done (this repo):**
- Bitwarden secret key references in tfvars
- `locals.tf` aggregation and instance normalisation

file.content:
---
name: Google Workspace
description: Read-only Gmail and Contacts access with configurable Calendar (readonly or readwrite) for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      env:
        - GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY
        - GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID
        - GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET
      bins:
        - google-workspace
    emoji: "📧"
    homepage: https://github.com/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---

# Google Workspace Skill

Access Gmail (read-only), Google Calendar (configurable), and Google Contacts (read-only).

## Important

- Gmail is strictly read-only. You cannot send, modify, or delete emails.
- Contacts is strictly read-only. You cannot create, modify, or delete contacts.
- Calendar access depends on the configured mode. Check with `google-workspace config show`.

## Check configuration

Before using any commands, verify what is enabled:

```
google-workspace config show
```

## Gmail commands

Search messages:
```
google-workspace gmail search --query "from:someone@example.com" --max-results 10
```

Read a message by ID:
```
google-workspace gmail read --id MESSAGE_ID
```

List labels:
```
google-workspace gmail labels
```

Search or read threads:
```
google-workspace gmail threads --query "subject:meeting"
google-workspace gmail threads --id THREAD_ID
```

## Calendar commands

List available calendars:
```
google-workspace calendar list
```

List upcoming events:
```
google-workspace calendar events --from 2026-03-29T00:00:00Z --to 2026-04-05T23:59:59Z
```

Get a specific event:
```
google-workspace calendar event --id EVENT_ID
```

Create an event (only if calendar mode is readwrite):
```
google-workspace calendar create --summary "Team sync" --start "2026-04-01T10:00:00Z" --end "2026-04-01T11:00:00Z"
```

Update an event (only if calendar mode is readwrite):
```
google-workspace calendar update --id EVENT_ID --summary "Updated title"
```

Delete an event (only if calendar mode is readwrite):
```
google-workspace calendar delete --id EVENT_ID
```

## Contacts commands

List contacts:
```
google-workspace contacts list --max-results 50
```

Search contacts:
```
google-workspace contacts search --query "John"
```

Get a specific contact:
```
google-workspace contacts get --id "people/c1234567890"
```

## Authentication status

Check if the token is valid:
```
google-workspace auth status
```

If the token has expired, ask the operator to re-authenticate by running `google-workspace auth login` on the host.

## Output format

All commands output JSON by default. Use `--output text` for plain text where supported.

1	package oauth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6	
7		"golang.org/x/oauth2"
8		"golang.org/x/oauth2/google"
9	)
10	
11	// NewOAuthConfig builds an OAuth2 config for Google APIs using
12	// the Desktop app flow (redirect to localhost).
13	func NewOAuthConfig(clientID, clientSecret string, scopes []string) *oauth2.Config {
14		return &oauth2.Config{
15			ClientID:     clientID,
16			ClientSecret: clientSecret,
17			Endpoint:     google.Endpoint,
18			RedirectURL:  "http://localhost",
19			Scopes:       scopes,
20		}
21	}
22	
23	// InteractiveLogin performs the OAuth2 authorisation code flow for a headless
24	// server. It prints a URL for the user to visit, then reads the auth code
25	// from stdin.
26	func InteractiveLogin(ctx context.Context, cfg *oauth2.Config) (*oauth2.Token, error) {
27		url := cfg.AuthCodeURL("state", oauth2.AccessTypeOffline, oauth2.ApprovalForce)
28	
29		fmt.Println("Visit this URL in your browser to authorise access:")
30		fmt.Println()
31		fmt.Println("  " + url)
32		fmt.Println()
33		fmt.Print("Paste the authorisation code here: ")
34	
35		var code string
36		if _, err := fmt.Scan(&code); err != nil {
37			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading auth code: %w", err)
38		}
39	
40		token, err := cfg.Exchange(ctx, code)
41		if err != nil {
42			return nil, fmt.Errorf("exchanging auth code: %w", err)
43		}
44	
45		return token, nil
46	}
47

1	package crypto
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/aes"
5		"crypto/cipher"
6		"crypto/rand"
7		"crypto/sha256"
8		"errors"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11	
12		"golang.org/x/crypto/hkdf"
13	)
14	
15	const (
16		saltSize = 16
17		keySize  = 32 // AES-256
18	)
19	
20	// Encrypt encrypts plaintext using AES-256-GCM with a key derived via HKDF-SHA256.
21	// The passphrase is the raw key material (e.g. from GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY).
22	// Output format: salt (16 bytes) || nonce (12 bytes) || ciphertext+tag.
23	func Encrypt(plaintext []byte, passphrase string) ([]byte, error) {
24		if passphrase == "" {
25			return nil, errors.New("encryption key must not be empty")
26		}
27	
28		salt := make([]byte, saltSize)
29		if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, salt); err != nil {
30			return nil, fmt.Errorf("generating salt: %w", err)
31		}
32	
33		key, err := deriveKey(passphrase, salt)
34		if err != nil {
35			return nil, err
36		}
37	
38		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
39		if err != nil {
40			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating cipher: %w", err)
41		}
42	
43		gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
44		if err != nil {
45			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating GCM: %w", err)
46		}
47	
48		nonce := make([]byte, gcm.NonceSize())
49		if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
50			return nil, fmt.Errorf("generating nonce: %w", err)
51		}
52	
53		ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nil, nonce, plaintext, nil)
54	
55		// salt || nonce || ciphertext
56		out := make([]byte, 0, saltSize+len(nonce)+len(ciphertext))
57		out = append(out, salt...)
58		out = append(out, nonce...)
59		out = append(out, ciphertext...)
60	
61		return out, nil
62	}
63	
64	// Decrypt decrypts data produced by Encrypt.
65	func Decrypt(data []byte, passphrase string) ([]byte, error) {
66		if passphrase == "" {
67			return nil, errors.New("encryption key must not be empty")
68		}
69	
70		if len(data) < saltSize+12 { // salt + minimum nonce size
71			return nil, errors.New("ciphertext too short")
72		}
73	
74		salt := data[:saltSize]
75		key, err := deriveKey(passphrase, salt)
76		if err != nil {
77			return nil, err
78		}
79	
80		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
81		if err != nil {
82			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating cipher: %w", err)
83		}
84	
85		gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating GCM: %w", err)
88		}
89	
90		nonceSize := gcm.NonceSize()
91		if len(data) < saltSize+nonceSize {
92			return nil, errors.New("ciphertext too short for nonce")
93		}
94	
95		nonce := data[saltSize : saltSize+nonceSize]
96		ciphertext := data[saltSize+nonceSize:]
97	
98		plaintext, err := gcm.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, nil)
99		if err != nil {
100			return nil, fmt.Errorf("decryption failed (wrong key or tampered data): %w", err)
101		}
102	
103		return plaintext, nil
104	}
105	
106	func deriveKey(passphrase string, salt []byte) ([]byte, error) {
107		r := hkdf.New(sha256.New, []byte(passphrase), salt, []byte("google-workspace-token"))
108		key := make([]byte, keySize)
109		if _, err := io.ReadFull(r, key); err != nil {
110			return nil, fmt.Errorf("deriving key: %w", err)
111		}
112		return key, nil
113	}
114

46		}

48	}

content.content:
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6	
7		"github.com/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/google-workspace/internal/config"
8		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
9	)
10	
11	var configCmd = &cobra.Command{
12		Use:   "config",
13		Short: "Manage service scope configuration",
14	}
15	
16	var configShowCmd = &cobra.Command{
17		Use:   "show",
18		Short: "Display current configuration",
19		Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
20			cfg, err := config.Load(configDir)
21			if err != nil {
22				exitf("loading config: %v", err)
23			}
24	
25			data, _ := json.MarshalIndent(cfg, "", "  ")
26			fmt.Println(string(data))
27			fmt.Printf("\nOAuth scopes: %v\n", cfg.OAuthScopes())
28		},
29	}
30	
31	var (
32		setGmail    *bool
33		setCalendar string
34		setContacts *bool
35	)
36	
37	var configSetCmd = &cobra.Command{
38		Use:   "set",
39		Short: "Update service scope configuration",
40		Long:  "Update which Google services are enabled and their access level.\nAfter changing scopes, re-run 'google-workspace auth login' for the change to take effect.",
41		Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
42			cfg, err := config.Load(configDir)
43			if err != nil {
44				exitf("loading config: %v", err)
45			}
46	
47			if cmd.Flags().Changed("gmail") {
48				cfg.Gmail = *setGmail
49			}
50			if cmd.Flags().Changed("calendar") {
51				cfg.Calendar = config.CalendarMode(setCalendar)
52			}
53			if cmd.Flags().Changed("contacts") {
54				cfg.Contacts = *setContacts
55			}
56	
57			if err := config.Save(configDir, cfg); err != nil {
58				exitf("saving config: %v", err)
59			}
60	
61			data, _ := json.MarshalIndent(cfg, "", "  ")
62			fmt.Println(string(data))
63			fmt.Println("\nConfig saved. Run 'google-workspace auth login' if scopes have changed.")
64		},
65	}
66	
67	func init() {
68		setGmail = configSetCmd.Flags().Bool("gmail", true, "enable Gmail read-only access")
69		configSetCmd.Flags().StringVar(&setCalendar, "calendar", "readonly", "calendar mode: off, readonly, or readwrite")
70		setContacts = configSetCmd.Flags().Bool("contacts", true, "enable Contacts read-only access")
71	
72		configCmd.AddCommand(configShowCmd, configSetCmd)
73		rootCmd.AddCommand(configCmd)
74	}
75

input.description: Build zoho-mail OpenClaw skill in Go
input.prompt:
Build a new Go OpenClaw skill called `zoho-mail` at `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/zoho-mail/`. This skill provides full read/write access to a Zoho Mail account for an AI agent.

## Must mirror the google-workspace skill structure exactly

The existing google-workspace skill is at `/home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/google-workspace/`. Read ALL its files before writing a single line. The zoho-mail skill must follow the same file layout, package naming conventions, error handling style, and cobra command patterns.

## Key structural differences from google-workspace

1. **No Google-specific packages** — use `golang.org/x/oauth2` with a custom Zoho endpoint (no `golang.org/x/oauth2/google`, no `google.golang.org/api`)
2. **No config modes** — Zoho skill is always full read/write; no `config set --mail=true` toggle needed. Config only stores the user's email address.
3. **HTTP-based API client** — Zoho Mail uses a REST API, not a Go SDK. Build a plain `net/http` client using the OAuth2 token source.
4. **HKDF label** — change `"google-workspace-token"` to `"zoho-mail-token"` in `internal/crypto/encrypt.go`

## Files to create

```
zoho-mail/
├── SKILL.md
├── main.go
├── go.mod
├── cmd/
│   ├── root.go
│   ├── auth.go
│   ├── config.go
│   ├── mail.go
│   └── folders.go
└── internal/
    ├── crypto/
    │   └── encrypt.go
    ├── oauth/
    │   ├── oauth.go
    │   └── token.go
    └── zoho/
        └── client.go
```

## Zoho OAuth details (EU data centre)

```go
endpoint := oauth2.Endpoint{
    AuthURL:  "https://accounts.zoho.eu/oauth/v2/auth",
    TokenURL: "https://accounts.zoho.eu/oauth/v2/token",
}
```

Scopes: `ZohoMail.messages.ALL`, `ZohoMail.folders.ALL`, `ZohoMail.accounts.READ`

For the interactive login: print the auth URL, ask the user to paste the **full redirect URL** (e.g. `http://localhost:8080/callback?code=xxxxx&location=eu`) back into the terminal. Extract the `code` query parameter from it. This avoids needing to start a local HTTP server.

```go
fmt.Print("Paste the full redirect URL here: ")
var redirectURL string
fmt.Scan(&redirectURL)
u, _ := url.Parse(redirectURL)
code := u.Query().Get("code")
```

Redirect URL to use in oauth2.Config: `http://localhost:8080/callback` (user must register this in Zoho API console).

## Environment variables

- `ZOHO_CLIENT_ID`
- `ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET`
- `ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY`

Default credential dir: `~/.openclaw/credentials/zoho-mail`

## Config (config.json in credential dir)

Just one field:
```go
type Config struct {
    Email string `json:"email"`
}
```

`config set --email=foo@example.com` saves it. `config show` displays it.

## Zoho Mail REST API client (`internal/zoho/client.go`)

Base URL: `https://mail.zoho.eu/api`

Use `oauth2.NewClient(ctx, tokenSource)` as the underlying HTTP client.

On first use, call `GET /accounts` to discover the accountId for the configured email, cache it in memory.

API response wrapper:
```go
type apiResponse struct {
    Status struct {
        Code        int    `json:"code"`
        Description string `json:"description"`
    } `json:"status"`
    Data json.RawMessage `json:"data"`
}
```

Check `status.code != 200` as an error condition.

Implement these methods on `Client`:

```go
// Account discovery
func (c *Client) AccountID(ctx context.Context) (string, error)

// Messages
func (c *Client) ListMessages(ctx context.Context, folderID string, limit, start int) ([]Message, error)
func (c *Client) GetMessage(ctx context.Context, msgID string) (MessageDetail, error)
func (c *Client) SendMessage(ctx context.Context, req SendRequest) (Message, error)
func (c *Client) DeleteMessage(ctx context.Context, msgID, folderID string) error
func (c *Client) MarkRead(ctx context.Context, msgIDs []string, isRead bool) error
func (c *Client) SearchMessages(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]Message, error)

// Folders
func (c *Client) ListFolders(ctx context.Context) ([]Folder, error)
```

Types:
```go
type Message struct {
    MessageID string `json:"messageId"`
    Subject   string `json:"subject"`
    FromAddress string `json:"fromAddress"`
    ToAddress string `json:"toAddress"`
    ReceivedTime string `json:"receivedTime"`
    FolderID string `json:"folderId"`
    IsRead bool `json:"isRead"`
    Summary string `json:"summary"`
}

type MessageDetail struct {
    Message
    Content string `json:"content"`
    HTMLContent string `json:"htmlContent"`
}

type SendRequest struct {
    FromAddress string `json:"fromAddress"`
    ToAddress   string `json:"toAddress"`
    CCAddress   string `json:"ccAddress,omitempty"`
    Subject     string `json:"subject"`
    Content     string `json:"content"`
    MailFormat  string `json:"mailFormat"` // "html" or "plaintext"
}

type Folder struct {
    FolderID   string `json:"folderId"`
    FolderName string `json:"folderName"`
    FolderType string `json:"folderType"`
    MessageCount int `json:"messageCount"`
    UnreadCount  int `json:"unreadCount"`
}
```

Zoho API endpoints:
- `GET /accounts` → list accounts → find accountId matching config email
- `GET /accounts/{id}/messages/view?folderId={folderId}&limit={limit}&start={start}`
- `GET /accounts/{id}/messages/{msgId}` 
- `POST /accounts/{id}/messages` → send
- `DELETE /accounts/{id}/messages/{msgId}?folderId={folderId}`
- `PUT /accounts/{id}/messages` with body `{"isRead": true/false, "messageId": ["id1",...]}` for mark read
- `GET /accounts/{id}/messages/search?searchKey={query}&searchLimit={limit}`
- `GET /accounts/{id}/folders`

## cmd/mail.go commands

- `zoho-mail mail list [--folder INBOX] [--limit 20] [--start 0]`
- `zoho-mail mail read --id MSG_ID`
- `zoho-mail mail send --to addr --subject text --body text [--cc addr] [--html]`
- `zoho-mail mail reply --id MSG_ID --body text [--html]` (get original message first, construct reply)
- `zoho-mail mail search --query text [--limit 20]`
- `zoho-mail mail delete --id MSG_ID --folder FOLDER_ID`
- `zoho-mail mail mark --ids id1,id2 --read` / `--unread`

## cmd/folders.go

- `zoho-mail folders list`

## cmd/auth.go

- `zoho-mail auth login` — interactive OAuth
- `zoho-mail auth status` — show token status
- `zoho-mail auth revoke` — delete stored token

## SKILL.md

Follow the google-workspace SKILL.md format exactly:
```yaml
---
name: Zoho Mail
description: Full read/write Zoho Mail access for OpenClaw agents
version: 0.1.0
author: panthrocorp
license: MIT-0
metadata:
  openclaw:
    requires:
      env:
        - ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY
        - ZOHO_CLIENT_ID
        - ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET
      bins:
        - zoho-mail
    emoji: "📬"
    homepage: https://github.com/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills
    os: ["linux"]
---
```

Document all commands with examples.

## go.mod

Module: `github.com/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/zoho-mail`
Go version: `1.26.1`
Dependencies (same versions as google-workspace):
- `github.com/spf13/cobra v1.10.2`
- `golang.org/x/crypto v0.49.0`
- `golang.org/x/oauth2 v0.36.0`

## Quality requirements

- British English in all comments and messages
- No `fmt.Println` for errors — use `exitf()`
- All errors wrapped with context using `fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err)`
- `printJSON()` helper in root or mail.go for consistent JSON output
- No magic strings — use constants for API base URL, default folder names, etc.
- `go.sum` does NOT need to be created — run `go mod tidy` after writing all files

After writing all files, run:
```bash
cd /home/listellm/repos/panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/zoho-mail && go mod tidy && go build ./...
```

Fix any compilation errors before finishing.

The operator must run `zoho-mail auth login` once after deployment to complete the OAuth flow. ## Check configuration ``` zoho-mail config show ``` ## Mail commands List messages in a folder (default: INBOX): ``` zoho-mail mail list zoho-mail mail list --folder INBOX --limit 20 --start 0 ``` Read a message by ID: ``` zoho-mail mail read --id MESSAGE_ID ``` Send a new message: ``` zoho-mail mail send --to recipient@example.com --subject "Hello" --body "Message body" zoho-mail mail send --to recipient@example.com --subject "Hello" --body "

HTML body

" --html zoho-mail mail send --to recipient@example.com --cc cc@example.com --subject "Hello" --body "Body" ``` Reply to a message: ``` zoho-mail mail reply --id MESSAGE_ID --body "Reply text" zoho-mail mail reply --id MESSAGE_ID --body "

HTML reply

" --html ``` Search messages: ``` zoho-mail mail search --query "from:someone@example.com" zoho-mail mail search --query "subject:invoice" --limit 20 ``` Delete a message: ``` zoho-mail mail delete --id MESSAGE_ID --folder FOLDER_ID ``` Mark messages as read or unread: ``` zoho-mail mail mark --ids id1,id2,id3 --read zoho-mail mail mark --ids id1 --unread ``` ## Folder commands List all folders: ``` zoho-mail folders list ``` ## Authentication commands Complete the OAuth flow (interactive, run by operator): ``` zoho-mail auth login ``` Check authentication status: ``` zoho-mail auth status ``` Delete the stored token: ``` zoho-mail auth revoke ``` ## Output format All commands output JSON by default. ## Setup (operator, one-time per instance) 1. Set the account email address: ``` zoho-mail config set --email trevor@example.com ``` 2. Authenticate: ``` zoho-mail auth login ``` Visit the printed URL, authenticate, then paste the full redirect URL back into the terminal.

content.text:
Skill builds cleanly. Here's a summary of what's now in both repos:

**`panthrocorp/openclaw-skills/zoho-mail/`** (new skill):
- Full Go CLI mirroring the google-workspace structure
- `auth login/status/revoke` — OAuth2 against `accounts.zoho.eu`
- `config set/show` — stores the email address
- `mail list/read/send/reply/search/delete/mark` — full read/write
- `folders list`
- AES-256-GCM token encryption at rest with HKDF (`zoho-mail-token` label)
- Token refresh is persisted back to disk automatically on each API call
- Reads `ZOHO_CLIENT_ID`, `ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET`, `ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY` from env — no vault coupling

**`panthrocorp/openclaw` Terraform** (already done earlier):
- Bitwarden → SSM → `.env` pipeline for the three secrets
- `credentials/zoho-mail/` directory created on deploy
- `panthrocorp-zoho-mail` installed via clawhub on every deploy

Next step before this is functional: create the Zoho API client at `api-console.zoho.eu` and store the three secrets in Bitwarden SM.

message.content: store with what keys?

content.text:
From the tfvars file already committed:

- `ZOHO_CLIENT_ID_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_LISTELL`
- `ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_LISTELL`
- `ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_LISTELL`

Generate the token key with:
```bash
openssl rand -hex 32
```

message.content: steps to create the api client?

content.text:
1. Go to `https://api-console.zoho.eu/`
2. Sign in with the Zoho account that owns Trevor's mailbox
3. Click **Add Client**
4. Select **Server-based Applications**
5. Fill in:
   - **Client Name**: `openclaw-trevor` (or similar)
   - **Homepage URL**: `https://openclaw.listell.co.uk`
   - **Authorized Redirect URIs**: `http://localhost:8080/callback`
6. Click **Create**
7. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the resulting screen

That's it. Store those two values plus the generated token key in Bitwarden SM, then `terraform apply`.

message.content: its asking for javascript domain

content.text: That's the field for browser-based (implicit) flows — you can put anything there for a Server-based app, it's not used. Enter `https://openclaw.listell.co.uk` and move on.

message.content: openclaw-trevor is an invalid name

content.text: Use only alphanumerics and hyphens — try `openclaw` or `trevor-mail`.

message.content: openclaw worked

content.text:
Good. Grab the Client ID and Client Secret from that screen and store them in Bitwarden SM with the keys:

- `ZOHO_CLIENT_ID_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_LISTELL`
- `ZOHO_CLIENT_SECRET_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_LISTELL`

Then generate and store the token key:
```bash
openssl rand -hex 32
```
Store the output as `ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_LISTELL`.

Once all three are in Bitwarden, `terraform apply` and we're ready to deploy.

message.content: generate me the token key

content.text: Store that value as `ZOHO_MAIL_TOKEN_KEY_OPENCLAW_AWS_SANDBOX_LISTELL` in Bitwarden SM.

message.content: all done

content.text:
Ready to apply. Run:

```bash
cd terraform
source ../.env.bitwarden
AWS_PROFILE=panthrocorp-openclaw terraform apply \
  -var-file=variables/listell/sandbox-openclaw/sandbox-openclaw.tfvars
```

That will create the three SSM parameters. Once merged and deployed via the GitHub Actions workflow, the next step will be the one-time OAuth setup on the instance.

content.text:
# gh-ship: Branch → Commit → PR → CI → Merge → CI

> **GitHub repos only.** Before doing anything, confirm the repo has a GitHub remote (`git remote -v`).
> If the remote is Azure DevOps or anything other than `github.com`, stop and tell the user this command does not apply — use the ADO MCP tools instead.

Ship the current working changes end-to-end on GitHub. Run each phase in order, stopping if anything fails.

## Phase 1: Branch

Check current branch with `git branch --show-current`.

- If on `main` or `master`: create a feature branch. Derive the name from the staged changes or `` if provided. Use kebab-case, prefixed with `fix/` or `feat/` per commit convention. Ask if unclear.
- If already on a feature branch: continue.

## Phase 2: Commit

Run `git status` and `git diff --cached` to understand what is staged.

- If nothing is staged: run `git diff --stat` to show unstaged changes, then ask which files to stage.
- Stage and commit following the project commit convention (semantic: `fix:`, `feat:`, `breaking:`; subject ≤50 chars; bullet body with emojis). Do not skip pre-commit hooks (`--no-verify` is forbidden unless the user explicitly requests it).
- If pre-commit hooks modify files, re-stage and commit again.

## Phase 3: Push & PR

Push the branch: `git push -u origin <branch>`.

Create the PR using `gh pr create`. Follow the project PR format:
- Short title (≤70 chars)
- Body with `## Summary` bullets and no test plan section
- Add `🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)` footer

Show the PR URL.

## Phase 4: CI Check (post-PR)

Wait for CI to complete: `gh run list --branch <branch> --limit 5` then watch the most recent run with `gh run watch <run-id>`.

- If CI passes: proceed to Phase 5.
- If CI fails: show the failed job logs (`gh run view <run-id> --log-failed`), diagnose, fix, commit, push. Re-run Phase 4.

## Phase 5: Merge

Once CI is green: `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch` (use squash unless the user specifies otherwise).

Confirm the merge completed and the branch was deleted.

## Phase 6: CI Check (post-merge)

Run `gh run list --branch main --limit 3` and watch the post-merge run.

- If CI passes: done. Report the merged PR URL and final status.
- If CI fails: report the failure with logs. Do not attempt auto-fix on main — flag it for the user.

## Notes

- `` can provide a branch name, PR title hint, or both.
- Never force-push, never skip hooks, never push directly to main/master.
- If at any point the user needs to take a manual action, stop and clearly state what is needed.

@@ -221,16 +221,31 @@ APPROVALS_PASSWORD=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/approvals-pas
 GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/google-workspace-token-key" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
 GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-id" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
 GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-secret" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
+GITHUB_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/github-token" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
 
 # Write container env file
-printf 'OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\nOPENCLAW_DOMAIN=%s\nAWS_REGION=%s\nINSTANCE_NAME=%s\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=%s\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=%s\nDISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=%s\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=%s\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=%s\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=%s\n' \
-  "$GATEWAY_TOKEN" "$DOMAIN" "$AWS_REGION" "$INSTANCE_NAME" "$LLM_API_KEY" "$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN_VAL" "$DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN_VAL" "$GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY" "$GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID" "$GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env
+printf 'OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\nOPENCLAW_DOMAIN=%s\nAWS_REGION=%s\nINSTANCE_NAME=%s\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=%s\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=%s\nDISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=%s\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=%s\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=%s\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=%s\nGH_TOKEN=%s\n' \
+  "$GATEWAY_TOKEN" "$DOMAIN" "$AWS_REGION" "$INSTANCE_NAME" "$LLM_API_KEY" "$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN_VAL" "$DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN_VAL" "$GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY" "$GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID" "$GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET" "$GITHUB_TOKEN" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env
 
 # Write approvals env file
 printf 'APPROVALS_PASSWORD=%s\nDEVICES_DIR=%s/config/devices\n' \
   "$APPROVALS_PASSWORD" "$OPENCLAW_HOME" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals
 chmod 600 $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals
 
+# Install gh CLI into config/bin so Trevor can use it inside the openclaw-gateway container
+GH_VERSION=$(curl -fsSL 'https://api.github.com/repos/cli/cli/releases/latest' | grep '"tag_name"' | cut -d'"' -f4 | sed 's/^v//') || true
+if [[ -n "$GH_VERSION" ]]; then
+  curl -fsSL "https://github.com/cli/cli/releases/download/v$GH_VERSION/gh_$${GH_VERSION}_linux_arm64.tar.gz" -o /tmp/gh.tar.gz
+  tar -xzf /tmp/gh.tar.gz -C /tmp
+  mv "/tmp/gh_$${GH_VERSION}_linux_arm64/bin/gh" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/gh"
+  rm -rf /tmp/gh.tar.gz "/tmp/gh_$${GH_VERSION}_linux_arm64"
+  echo "gh $GH_VERSION installed to $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/"
+else
+  echo "WARNING: gh CLI install skipped, could not determine version"
+fi
+
+chmod +x $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/* 2>/dev/null || true
+
 # Set ownership
 chown -R openclaw:openclaw $OPENCLAW_HOME
 chown -R 1000:1000 $OPENCLAW_HOME/config $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace
@@ -244,13 +259,13 @@ docker image prune -af
 # Wait for gateway to be healthy before installing skills
 for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker exec openclaw-gateway curl -sf http://localhost:18789/health && break || sleep 2; done
 
-# Install clawhub skills

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
exec > /var/log/user-data.log 2>&1

# 1. Install Docker (official repo) and AWS CLI v2
apt-get update
DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupg

install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc
chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc

echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] \
  https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$${VERSION_CODENAME}") stable" \
  > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list

apt-get update
DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-compose-plugin

systemctl enable docker
systemctl start docker

# Install AWS CLI v2
curl -fsSL "https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-aarch64.zip" -o /tmp/awscliv2.zip
apt-get install -y unzip
unzip -q /tmp/awscliv2.zip -d /tmp
/tmp/aws/install
rm -rf /tmp/awscliv2.zip /tmp/aws

# Install Amazon CloudWatch Agent (non-fatal, observability should not block runtime)
curl -fsSL "https://amazoncloudwatch-agent.s3.amazonaws.com/ubuntu/arm64/1.300050.0/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb" -o /tmp/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb \
  && dpkg -i /tmp/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb \
  || echo "WARNING: CloudWatch Agent install failed, continuing without monitoring"
rm -f /tmp/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.deb

# 2. Mount data EBS volume (format only on first boot)
# Nitro/Graviton instances expose EBS as NVMe; find the unpartitioned 20G disk
DATA_DEVICE=$(lsblk -dpno NAME,SIZE,TYPE | awk '$2 == "20G" && $3 == "disk" {print $1}')
DATA_MOUNT="/home/openclaw"

if [[ -z "$${DATA_DEVICE}" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Could not find 20G data EBS volume" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if ! blkid "$${DATA_DEVICE}" | grep -q ext4; then
  mkfs.ext4 "$${DATA_DEVICE}"
fi

mkdir -p "$${DATA_MOUNT}"
mount "$${DATA_DEVICE}" "$${DATA_MOUNT}"

# Use UUID in fstab so the mount survives NVMe device reordering across reboots
DATA_UUID=$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$${DATA_DEVICE}")
grep -q "$${DATA_MOUNT}" /etc/fstab || echo "UUID=$${DATA_UUID} $${DATA_MOUNT} ext4 defaults,nofail 0 2" >> /etc/fstab

# 3. Create openclaw user
id openclaw &>/dev/null || useradd -m -s /bin/bash openclaw
usermod -aG docker openclaw
usermod -aG docker ubuntu

# 4. Create directory structure
mkdir -p /home/openclaw/config /home/openclaw/workspace
chown -R openclaw:openclaw /home/openclaw

# 5. Install Twingate EC2 connector (host-level systemd service)
IMDS_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X PUT "http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token" \
  -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 60")
AWS_REGION=$(curl -s -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $IMDS_TOKEN" \
  http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/placement/region)

TWINGATE_NETWORK=$(aws ssm get-parameter \
  --name "${twingate_ssm_network_name}" \
  --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  --with-decryption \
  --query 'Parameter.Value' \
  --output text)

TG_EC2_ACCESS_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter \
  --name "${twingate_ec2_ssm_access_token_name}" \
  --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  --with-decryption \
  --query 'Parameter.Value' \
  --output text)

TG_EC2_REFRESH_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter \
  --name "${twingate_ec2_ssm_refresh_token_name}" \
  --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  --with-decryption \
  --query 'Parameter.Value' \
  --output text)

if [[ -z "$TWINGATE_NETWORK" || -z "$TG_EC2_ACCESS_TOKEN" || -z "$TG_EC2_REFRESH_TOKEN" ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: Failed to retrieve Twingate EC2 connector parameters from SSM" >&2
  exit 1
fi

curl -fsSL "https://binaries.twingate.com/connector/setup.sh" | \
  TWINGATE_ACCESS_TOKEN="$TG_EC2_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  TWINGATE_REFRESH_TOKEN="$TG_EC2_REFRESH_TOKEN" \
  TWINGATE_NETWORK="$TWINGATE_NETWORK" \
  TWINGATE_LABEL_DEPLOYED_BY="linux" \
  bash


# 6. Update Route53 A record with private IP (Twingate resolves via its DNS proxy)
DOMAIN="${domain}"
PRIVATE_IP=$(curl -s -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $IMDS_TOKEN" \
  http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/local-ipv4)
HOSTED_ZONE_ID=$(aws route53 list-hosted-zones-by-name \
  --dns-name "$DOMAIN" \
  --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  --query 'HostedZones[0].Id' \
  --output text | sed 's|/hostedzone/||')

aws route53 change-resource-record-sets \
  --hosted-zone-id "$HOSTED_ZONE_ID" \
  --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  --change-batch "{
    \"Changes\": [{
      \"Action\": \"UPSERT\",
      \"ResourceRecordSet\": {
        \"Name\": \"$DOMAIN\",
        \"Type\": \"A\",
        \"TTL\": 60,
        \"ResourceRecords\": [{\"Value\": \"$PRIVATE_IP\"}]
      }
    }]
  }"

# 7. Obtain TLS certificate (persist on data volume to survive instance replacement)
CERT_DIR=/home/openclaw/letsencrypt
mkdir -p "$CERT_DIR"
DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get install -y certbot python3-certbot-dns-route53

if [[ ! -d "$CERT_DIR/live/$DOMAIN" ]]; then
  certbot certonly \
    --dns-route53 \
    -d "$DOMAIN" \
    --non-interactive \
    --agree-tos \
    --email "noreply@panthrocorp.co.uk" \
    --config-dir "$CERT_DIR" \
    --work-dir /tmp/certbot \
    --logs-dir /var/log/letsencrypt
fi

# 8. Create renewal deploy hook and systemd timer
mkdir -p "$CERT_DIR/renewal-hooks/deploy"
cat > "$CERT_DIR/renewal-hooks/deploy/reload-caddy.sh" << 'HOOK'
#!/bin/bash
docker exec caddy caddy reload --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile 2>/dev/null || true
HOOK
chmod 755 "$CERT_DIR/renewal-hooks/deploy/reload-caddy.sh"

cat > /etc/systemd/system/certbot-renewal.service << UNIT
[Unit]
Description=certbot renewal
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/bin/certbot renew --config-dir $CERT_DIR --work-dir /tmp/certbot --logs-dir /var/log/letsencrypt
UNIT

cat > /etc/systemd/system/certbot-renewal.timer << 'TIMER'
[Unit]
Description=certbot renewal timer

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*-*-* 02:30:00
RandomizedDelaySec=3600
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
TIMER

systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable --now certbot-renewal.timer

# 9. Deploy application from S3
OPENCLAW_HOME="$DATA_MOUNT"
CONFIG_BUCKET="${config_bucket_name}"
INSTANCE_NAME="${instance_name}"

aws s3 sync "s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/" \
  --region "$AWS_REGION" \
  --delete \
  --exclude '.env*' \
  --exclude 'config/agents/*' \
  --exclude 'config/credentials/*' \
  --exclude 'config/devices/*' \
  --exclude 'config/logs/*' \
  --exclude 'config/sessions/*' \
  --exclude 'config/identity/*' \
  --exclude 'workspace/*' \
  --exclude 'letsencrypt/*'

mkdir -p $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/{devices,logs,cron,canvas,sessions,identity,bin,credentials/google-workspace} $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace

# Deploy managed workspace files from S3
aws s3 cp "s3://$CONFIG_BUCKET/$INSTANCE_NAME/workspace/MODEL_GUIDE.md" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/MODEL_GUIDE.md" --region "$AWS_REGION" 2>/dev/null || true

# Start CloudWatch Agent if config exists
if [[ -f "$OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json" ]]; then
  /opt/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/bin/amazon-cloudwatch-agent-ctl \
    -a fetch-config -m ec2 -s \
    -c "file:$OPENCLAW_HOME/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.json"
fi

# Install approvals systemd service
cp $OPENCLAW_HOME/approvals/openclaw-approvals.service /etc/systemd/system/

# Fetch secrets from SSM
GATEWAY_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/gateway-token" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text)
LLM_API_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/llm-api-key" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN_VAL=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/telegram-bot-token" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN_VAL=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/discord-bot-token" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
APPROVALS_PASSWORD=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/approvals-password" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/google-workspace-token-key" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-id" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/google-client-secret" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')
GITHUB_TOKEN=$(aws ssm get-parameter --name "/$INSTANCE_NAME/github-token" --region "$AWS_REGION" --with-decryption --query Parameter.Value --output text 2>/dev/null || echo '')

# Write container env file
printf 'OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=%s\nOPENCLAW_DOMAIN=%s\nAWS_REGION=%s\nINSTANCE_NAME=%s\nOPENROUTER_API_KEY=%s\nTELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=%s\nDISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=%s\nGOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY=%s\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=%s\nGOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=%s\nGH_TOKEN=%s\n' \
  "$GATEWAY_TOKEN" "$DOMAIN" "$AWS_REGION" "$INSTANCE_NAME" "$LLM_API_KEY" "$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN_VAL" "$DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN_VAL" "$GOOGLE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN_KEY" "$GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID" "$GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET" "$GITHUB_TOKEN" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env

# Write approvals env file
printf 'APPROVALS_PASSWORD=%s\nDEVICES_DIR=%s/config/devices\n' \
  "$APPROVALS_PASSWORD" "$OPENCLAW_HOME" > $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals
chmod 600 $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env $OPENCLAW_HOME/.env.approvals

# Install gh CLI into config/bin so Trevor can use it inside the openclaw-gateway container
GH_VERSION=$(curl -fsSL 'https://api.github.com/repos/cli/cli/releases/latest' | grep '"tag_name"' | cut -d'"' -f4 | sed 's/^v//') || true
if [[ -n "$GH_VERSION" ]]; then
  curl -fsSL "https://github.com/cli/cli/releases/download/v$GH_VERSION/gh_$${GH_VERSION}_linux_arm64.tar.gz" -o /tmp/gh.tar.gz
  tar -xzf /tmp/gh.tar.gz -C /tmp
  mv "/tmp/gh_$${GH_VERSION}_linux_arm64/bin/gh" "$OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/gh"
  rm -rf /tmp/gh.tar.gz "/tmp/gh_$${GH_VERSION}_linux_arm64"
  echo "gh $GH_VERSION installed to $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/"
else
  echo "WARNING: gh CLI install skipped, could not determine version"
fi

chmod +x $OPENCLAW_HOME/config/bin/* 2>/dev/null || true

# Set ownership
chown -R openclaw:openclaw $OPENCLAW_HOME
chown -R 1000:1000 $OPENCLAW_HOME/config $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace

# Start containers
cd $OPENCLAW_HOME
sudo -u openclaw docker compose pull --quiet
sudo -u openclaw docker compose up -d --force-recreate
docker image prune -af

# Wait for gateway to be healthy before installing skills
for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker exec openclaw-gateway curl -sf http://localhost:18789/health && break || sleep 2; done

# Install clawhub skills (slugs without author prefix, --force for suspicious skills)
docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install panthrocorp-google-workspace --force 2>&1 || echo "WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for panthrocorp-google-workspace"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install git-essentials 2>&1 || echo "WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for git-essentials"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install markdown-formatter 2>&1 || echo "WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for markdown-formatter"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install markdown-converter --force 2>&1 || echo "WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for markdown-converter"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install exa-web-search-free --force 2>&1 || echo "WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for exa-web-search-free"
docker exec openclaw-gateway npx clawhub@latest install github-cli --force 2>&1 || echo "WARNING: clawhub skill install failed for github-cli"

# Start approvals service
systemctl daemon-reload
systemctl enable openclaw-approvals
systemctl restart openclaw-approvals

echo "Bootstrap and deploy complete."
